subject: Sociology
title: What Is Secularization?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Is Secularization?.

hook.text: In 1900, almost everyone went to church. By 2000, many pews sat empty. What happened?

buildup.text: Secularization theory argues that modernization inevitably reduces religion's influence on society.

discovery.text: It operates on three levels: decline in belief, loss of social influence, and privatization of faith.

twist.text: But secularization mostly describes Europe. The US stayed religious while modernizing rapidly.

climax.text: Secularization isn't a universal law. It's a pattern that fits some places and fails in others.
